typography.headings: JetBrains Mono, uppercase, tracking-[0.2em], text-[#00E5FF] or text-[#00FF66], font-bold
typography.kpis: JetBrains Mono, text-4xl or 5xl, font-black, high contrast
typography.labels: JetBrains Mono, text-xs, uppercase, tracking-wider, text-[#8892b0]

visual_enhancers.surface_treatment: Retro-Futurism/Terminal Look. Deep black/indigo backgrounds. High contrast borders. No soft glassmorphism. Use rigid 1px borders with glowing accents.
visual_enhancers.background_pattern: Faint dot-grid overlay across the main app background: `bg-[radial-gradient(rgba(0,255,102,0.15)_1px,transparent_1px)] bg-[size:24px_24px]`.
visual_enhancers.scanlines: Subtle repeating-linear-gradient overlay to mimic CRT/Terminal scanlines, kept at very low opacity to maintain readability.

page_1_unifi_devices.layout: Top Header with KPI cards. Main content area: Flex row, wrap, auto-sizing cards for each site.

kpi_header.style: Minimalist rectangular containers, solid 1px border, neon metric values, red for OFFLINE.

pcb_tree_card.style: Dark card background (`#0b0b14`), 1px primary green/cyan border, internal faint dot grid.
pcb_tree_card.hierarchy: Horizontal or Vertical tree. Root (Gateway) -> Switches -> APs/Cameras (leaves).
pcb_tree_card.connectors: Orthogonal elbow lines. Use SVG `<path d="M x1 y1 H x2 V y2" />` for strict 90-degree corners. Stroke color `#00FF66` (opacity 40%).
pcb_tree_card.nodes: Rectangular or pill-shaped. Dark bg, neon text. Display: Device Name, IP, Status Dot.
pcb_tree_card.status_dot: Green (`#00FF66`) if online, Red (`#FF2A2A`) if offline.
pcb_tree_card.offline_state: Node border turns `#FF2A2A`, text red, aggressive red pulsing glow.
pcb_tree_card.animation: Flowing trace animation on active connections. Add a secondary SVG `<path>` over the connector with `stroke-dasharray` and animate `stroke-dashoffset` using linear CSS animation. Keep it lightweight.

page_2_wug_topology.layout: Two structural areas: Top 65-70% height (Radial SVG Hub & Spoke), Bottom 30-35% height (Compact device roster).

radial_graph.center_node: 'WUG POLLER'. Styled with Cyan (`#00E5FF`). Hexagon or distinct shape. Emits sonar-like pulsing rings.
radial_graph.spokes: Straight SVG lines from center to 7-9 location nodes arrayed in a circle.
radial_graph.location_nodes: Rectangular terminal-style badge. Shows: Site Name, Device Count, X/Y UP badge.
radial_graph.animation: Data 'packet' flow. Since Pi GPU is disabled, use an array of static SVG `<circle>` elements spaced along the line, and use CSS `@keyframes` to pulse their `opacity` sequentially, creating the illusion of movement without heavy transform/path calculations.
radial_graph.offline_state: Entire location node border glows red. Radial spoke line turns red. Red sonar pulse emitting from the offline node. ALL text related to it turns red.

device_roster.style: Compact, dense data list/table mimicking a terminal output. Columns: SITE | TYPE | DEVICE | IP | STATUS.
device_roster.highlight: Offline devices are highlighted with a faint red background (`bg-[#FF2A2A]/10`) and red text for immediate visibility.

animations_and_performance.raspberry_pi_optimization: Use ONLY `opacity` and `transform` for animations. Avoid animating `box-shadow`, `width`, `height`, or `top/left` directly. For glowing pulses, animate the `opacity` of a pseudo-element (`::after`) that has the static box-shadow.

keyframes.pulse_red: Opacity toggle 0.3 to 1, or scale 1 to 1.05.
keyframes.sonar_ring: Scale from 1 to 2, opacity from 0.8 to 0 over 2-3 seconds.
keyframes.trace_flow: Linear animation of `stroke-dashoffset` from X to 0.

UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: 1. Strictly use JetBrains Mono for all text. Import it via Google Fonts if necessary (`@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;700;800&display=swap');`).
2. Ensure all key informational elements, nodes, and KPI blocks have a `data-testid` attribute (e.g., `data-testid="kpi-total-devices"`, `data-testid="node-wug-poller"`).
3. Use standard CSS files or inline Tailwind plugins to define the custom keyframes (`sonar-ping`, `trace-flow`).
4. DO NOT use complete `transparent` backgrounds for any overlay or card that has visual elements beneath it; use a solid dark hex with opacity (e.g., `#070710e6`).
5. Avoid standard Shadcn components if they overcomplicate the layout. A raw HTML/Tailwind table and custom SVG rendering is heavily preferred for the dashboard data visualizations.
6. Build the custom SVG graphs (PCB Tree and Radial Hub) directly in React components. Calculate the (X, Y) coordinates dynamically for the radial layout using `Math.cos` and `Math.sin`.
7. Do not use generic AI emojis anywhere. Use SVG icons (e.g., Phosphor Icons or Lucide) mapped to network concepts (Server, Router, Access Point) if icons are needed.
8. Maintain a strict 1920x1080 fixed/scaled layout mindset. Ensure `overflow-hidden` is applied correctly to avoid kiosk scrollbars unless intended for the roster list.
